tool: Siril 1.4.4 register (2-pass global star alignment) regdata via scripts/qa/inspect_stage.py; flagged by scripts/qa/cull_report.py
method: 373 NEF -> CFA FITS (undebayered) -> register -2pass in 5 disk-bounded batches of 75; per-frame FWHM/roundness/background/#stars from Siril findstar PSF fits, pooled (these metrics are reference-independent, so pooling across independently-registered batches is valid)
caveats: CFA-SAMPLED: frames were NOT debayered (to fit the arm base rig's ~6.8 GB free disk). Absolute FWHM is inflated by the Bayer mosaic and is NOT true stellar FWHM in arcsec -- only RELATIVE comparison across frames is valid. Removal condition: re-measure debayered on x86 (no disk limit).
wFWHM is batch-relative (each batch has its own reference) and is not a cull input.
Flags are CANDIDATES for a measured with/without cull ladder -- not an executed cull (repo stack-policy doctrine).

roundness.note: FWHMy/FWHMx; <1 = elongated (fixed-tripod trailing), uniform across the set

flagged_defect_side_z3p5.note: worst on all three axes (one-off disturbance); anomaly audit also flagged a streak here
brief 2-frame dip near session end; DSC_7265 immediately after is the best-focus frame (transient)
brief 2-frame dip near session end; DSC_7265 immediately after is the best-focus frame (transient)

assessment: Clean, stable ~43-min run: 373/373 registered (0 match-failed), 98% of frames within +-2% of median FWHM, sky background flat to +-0.3% (no cloud/glow/gradient drift), steady star counts (~1850), uniform ~0.615 roundness (inherent 6 s fixed-tripod trailing, not a per-frame defect). Only ~1% flagged and even the worst frame is ~6% wider than median -- dropping the 4 candidates is very likely a NULL on the stack.
